Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The current model starts with creating and approving the requisition in sequence then a parallel block containing the remaining activities followed by closing The problem here is that the PARALLEL operator allows all those activities to happen in any order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For example someone could pay the invoice before receiving the goods or even create the PO after receiving the goods Thats definitely not correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The PARALLEL block allows activities to execute in any order which is bad because the Procure to Pay process has a strict sequence For instance you must create the PO after approving the requisition then receive goods receive invoice match them pay then close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the standard Procure to Pay steps Create Requisition Approve Requisition Create PO Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The correct sequence should be in order from 3 to 7 after the initial two steps The current models PARALLEL block includes all these steps as parallel which is wrong They should be in a sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the fix would be to replace the PARALLEL operator with a SEQUENCE operator for the remaining activities after approval

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The given standard logic is a strict sequence so the process tree should enforce that sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the corrected process tree should have a SEQUENCE after the initial approve req containing the remaining activities in order create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice then close case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: By making them a SEQUENCE the correct order is enforced
